title: json_skip_container mismatches mixed bracket types
description: json_skip_container(chars, open, close) tracks depth by counting open and close characters of the SAME type as passed, ignoring the other. Input like [{]} would be accepted: the [ opens depth=1, { is ignored, ] closes depth=0 and returns Ok, even though the JSON is structurally invalid. Iterator position after the call is still correct (we've consumed the right characters) so in practice the outer parser produces the right result. — Suggested rewrite: use a proper depth stack (Vec<char>) that tracks which open bracket type was seen, and validates that the corresponding close bracket matches. Or document the limitation explicitly in the fn comment so a future contributor doesn't unknowingly rely on it for validation.
